# Instagram Post — NVIDIA Launches NemoClaw Enterprise AI Agent Platform at GTC 2026, Bets Big on Inference Revenue
# Week: Week of 2026-03-16

---

NVIDIA just launched the enterprise agent platform.

At GTC 2026, Jensen Huang revealed NemoClaw — an enterprise-grade AI agent platform built on OpenClaw, the open-source framework that lets companies build and run AI agents on their own hardware.

This is the moment agentic AI moves from demos to deployment. The two biggest barriers holding back enterprise adoption — data governance and infrastructure control — are addressed head-on. NemoClaw keeps your data on your servers while giving your teams the tools to build autonomous AI workflows.

NVIDIA is also betting that inference, not training, will be the dominant compute workload as agents scale. IBM announced a parallel collaboration focused on sovereign AI infrastructure with full regional data residency.

For CTOs and CEOs: the platform layer for enterprise agents is no longer a gap in the market. It is here. The question is how quickly your organization can architect around it.

GYanTech AI designs agentic AI strategies for enterprises ready to move. Link in bio.

## Fact-check notes
- NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang announced NemoClaw, an enterprise-grade AI agent platform built on the open-source OpenClaw framework, during the GTC 2026 keynote (Source: TechCrunch, March 16, 2026)
- NemoClaw enables enterprises to build and run AI agents locally on their own hardware (Source: TechCrunch, March 16, 2026)
- NVIDIA highlighted AI inference as a major chip revenue opportunity at GTC 2026 (Source: Reuters, March 16, 2026)
- NVIDIA GTC 2026 revealed the Vera Rubin architecture along with updated AI software platforms (Source: SaasNik / NVIDIA GTC, March 16, 2026)
- IBM announced expanded collaboration with NVIDIA at GTC 2026 to advance enterprise AI, including sovereign AI infrastructure with regional data residency (Source: IBM Newsroom, March 16, 2026)
